task_name: Maya Memory Binding: bluepilot to shared Block-2 memory
goal: Make bluepilot's Maya memory a thin client of the one shared Block-2 memory in maya-core, with local JSON only as offline fallback.
scope_out: Do not copy Block-2 memory logic or ethics/budget categories into bluepilot.
Do not let bluepilot write a confirmed entry directly.
Do not remove the local JSON fallback.
Do not bind persona/voice or ethics here.
claims: tools/maya-memory-remote-client.cjs calls maya-core via MAYA_CORE_URL and sends app_origin='bluepilot'.
tools/maya-memory.cjs uses the remote client as primary path and local JSON as offline fallback.
Writes are proposals only and never directly confirmed.
The four existing ALLOWED_KEYS still work through get, set, and list.
assumptions: maya-core exposes Block-2 memory endpoints compatible with POST /api/maya/memory and GET /api/maya/memory.
MAYA_CORE_URL and MAYA_CORE_GATE_TOKEN or MAYA_BUILDER_GATE_TOKEN will be configured in deployed environments.
Local JSON fallback remains acceptable when maya-core is unreachable.
dod: tools/maya-memory-remote-client.cjs provides proposeEntry, readConfirmed, and listConfirmed.
tools/maya-memory.cjs falls back to local JSON and marks fallback entries local/offline.
test-maya-memory-fixtures.cjs covers remote propose pending, remote read/list mapping, and offline fallback.
docs/BP_C3_MAYA_MEMORY_CONTRACT_v0.md states shared Block-2 memory is primary and local JSON is fallback only.
stop_conditions: Persona or ethics binding becomes necessary
A second memory schema becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-122 added docs/CLAUDE-CONTEXT.md; Maya binding plan says memory first because Bluepilot currently has a real memory tool but no speaking Maya surface.
